blocks.content: Now you'll create a time interval that defines when notifications should be suppressed.
To create a time interval, complete the following steps:

Click **New time interval**.

The time interval creation form opens.

Enter a descriptive **Name** for this time interval.

For example: `weekend-quiet-hours` or `friday-maintenance-window`. Use a name that clearly communicates when and why notifications are suppressed.

Enter the **Start time** in HH:MM format.

For example, `22:00` for 10 PM. This is when suppression begins each day the interval is active.

Enter the **End time** in HH:MM format.

For example, `06:00` for 6 AM. Together with the start time, this defines the daily suppression window.

Optionally, specify which **Days of the week** this interval applies to.

For example, enter `saturday, sunday` for weekend-only suppression. Leave blank to apply every day of the week.

Click **Save time interval** to create the interval.

The Time intervals page updates to show your new interval. It isn't active yet — you still need to attach it to a notification policy.
Your time interval is created. In the next milestone, you'll attach it to a notification policy as a mute timing so it starts suppressing notifications.
